scientific_claim: The study identified a large rice flag-leaf sRNA catalog from an IMF2 population
source_reported_content: 104 libraries yielded 53,613,739 unique sRNAs and 165,797 sRNA expression traits; 24-nt sRNAs were most numerous
method: sRNA-seq; read filtering; parental SNP-replaced mapping
source_location: Abstract; Results: Patterns and distributions of sRNAs

scientific_claim: sRNA abundance is extensively controlled by local and distant QTLs
source_reported_content: 66,649 s-traits mapped 40,049 local-sQTLs and 30,809 distant-sQTLs; 22,263 scQTLs were recovered for 20,249 sc-traits
limitation: QTL intervals require validation for causal variants

scientific_claim: Most sRNAs and their mother genes showed little co-regulation
source_reported_content: The abstract states that genetic co-regulation was observed for a portion of sRNAs, but most sRNAs and mother genes showed little co-regulation
limitation: does not define causal basis of decoupling

scientific_claim: Distant-sQTL hotspots may involve sRNA biogenesis genes
source_reported_content: Some sRNA biogenesis genes were located in distant-sQTL hotspots and corresponded with specific length classes of sRNAs
method: QTL hotspot analysis; candidate gene annotation
limitation: candidate genes not functionally validated in provided text

scientific_claim: sRNA levels showed widespread negative dominance in the IMF2 population
source_reported_content: The discussion reports heterozygotes often had lower sRNA levels than parental means or lower homozygotes, and negative-dominance sQTLs were mostly distant-sQTLs
